mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equity cash flows declines and financial conditions loosen, which supports S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. This signal is falsified if rate cuts are followed by flat or negative 20-day index returns.
failure_condition: If triggered easing periods do not outperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return and the sign accuracy stays below 55% over rolling 2-year windows, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates sharply, real policy rates fall behind inflation and investors price a higher future policy burden, which pushes S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The claim fails if inflation surges are not followed by weaker index performance.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is below CPI inflation
failure_condition: Reject if, after inflation spikes above the trigger, SPX posts positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or the average excess return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice near-term earnings risk. The signal is testable by comparing returns after unemployment spikes versus normal months.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if monthly unemployment increases of at least 0.2pp are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, payroll loss and rising slack jointly indicate a broad labor downturn that should hurt equity returns over the next 60 trading days through weaker consumption and profit growth. The mechanism fails if the labor-market combination does not precede weaker medium-horizon returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-loss and unemployment-rise regime does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue expectations for consumer-facing firms, which supports S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The hypothesis is falsified if retail-sales gains do not translate into positive short-horizon equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS trend observations are not associated with higher 20-day SPX returns than the baseline sample and the sign hit rate remains below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO signals improving industrial output and operating leverage, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and boost S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The claim fails if industrial production uptrends are followed by no medium-horizon equity strength.
failure_condition: Reject if, over rolling 3-year windows, positive INDPRO momentum does not correspond to higher 60-day SPX returns and Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST weakens and GDP growth also slows, the housing-demand channel and broader final demand both deteriorate, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is falsified if housing weakness with slower GDP does not coincide with equity drawdowns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months and GDPC1 growth is below its prior 4-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-and-GDP slowdown regime does not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a reopening rebound, an increase in INDPRO alongside a falling UNRATE signals stronger real activity and labor absorption, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is testable because it predicts better equity performance only when both production and labor improve together.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points in a month while INDPRO rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if, under the joint improve-economy trigger, 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average and the hit rate does not exceed 55%.
